title: Adversarial validation for race/test-shift weighting
summary: The train/test split likely contains meaningful covariate shift from year, preseason sessions, and unseen driver/race compositions.
rationale: The train/test split likely contains meaningful covariate shift from year, preseason sessions, and unseen driver/race compositions. Adversarial-validation literature shows offline CV can look optimistic when training rows are less test-like than deployment rows.
implementation_hint: Train a leak-free classifier to distinguish train vs test using raw covariates only. Use its score to: 1) reweight training rows toward test-like races, 2) build harsher validation folds from the most test-like races, or 3) prune obviously off-distribution rows. Compare against strict `GroupKFold` by `Race_Year` and optionally leave-one-year-out.
expected_effect: Reduces public-score surprises and may improve leaderboard robustness even if local CV changes little.
risk: If the shift is partly conditional-label shift, pure covariate reweighting can overfit to the unlabeled test distribution and hurt AUC.
